Theorem 1.11.

Assume that \(f\) is bounded on \([a, b]\) and that \(\alpha' \in \cR\) on \([a, b]\) (namely, \(\alpha'\) is Riemann integrable on \([a, b]\)). Then \(f\in \cR(\alpha)\) on \([a, b]\) iff \(f\alpha' \in \cR\) on \([a, b]\text{,}\) and
\begin{equation} \int_{a}^{b} f\, d\alpha = \int_{a}^{b} f \alpha'\, dx.\tag{1.2} \end{equation}
in-context
